author: denizokcu
description: Record audio and transcribe it to markdown using OpenAI Audio Transcription API. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Deniz Okcu
manifest.description: Capture voice notes, meetings, and daily-note recordings as reliable Markdown using OpenAI.
manifest.name: Voice MD

name: Voice MD

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Voice MD 1.4.0 — Mobile reliability and iOS Action Button capture

 Voice MD 1.4.0 turns the plugin from a simple “record → transcribe → insert” workflow into a more reliable mobile-first voice capture layer for Obsidian.

 This release focuses on safer mobile recording, retryable transcription jobs, daily-note capture from iOS Shortcuts, and clearer Markdown output.

 ### Highlights

 - Mobile reliability: stopped recordings are saved locally before transcription starts.
 - Retry queue: failed/offline transcriptions can be retried later.
 - iOS Shortcut / Action Button support: open today’s daily note and start recording from a URL.
 - Daily note appends: URL-triggered recordings append to the end of the target note under a time heading.
 - Raw transcript first: raw transcripts are saved before optional structured note generation.
 - SecretStorage support: API keys migrate to Obsidian SecretStorage when available.
 - OpenAI upload guard: recordings over the 25 MB transcription limit are blocked before upload.

 ### iOS Shortcut / Action Button

 You can now create an iOS Shortcut using Open URLs with:

 ```text
   obsidian://voice-md?record=true&daily=true&autostart=true
 ```

 Then assign that Shortcut to the iPhone Action Button.

 Voice MD will:

 1. Open Obsidian.
 2. Open or create today’s configured daily note.
 3. Wait for the editor.
 4. Start recording.
 5. Append the result at the end under a time heading, for example:

 ```md
   ## 14:32

   Captured voice note text...
 ```

 For multi-vault setups, include your vault name if needed:

 ```text
   obsidian://voice-md?vault=Your%20Vault&record=true&daily=true&autostart=true
 ```

 ### New settings

 - Retain failed audio — number of days to keep local audio for pending/failed retry jobs.
 - Daily note folder — folder used by daily-note URL shortcuts.
 - Daily note date format — date format used for daily-note URL shortcuts.
 - Use 24-hour time — controls timestamps for URL-appended recordings.

 ### Changed

 - Normal recordings now insert as a new paragraph at the cursor.
 - URL-triggered daily-note recordings append to the end of the target note.
 - Post-processing no longer uses a fixed completion token cap, reducing truncation risk for longer meeting notes.
 - Transcription output folder creation is more reliable.
 - Transcript file names now avoid collisions.
 - README has been rewritten around quick capture, mobile reliability, iOS shortcuts, and privacy.

 ### Fixed

 - Prevented settings-tab freezes related to API key / SecretStorage handling.
 - Added an OpenAI 25 MB transcription upload guard.
 - Improved browser/mobile network failure detection so failed jobs are retryable.
 - Post-processing failures no longer discard successful raw transcripts.

 ### Privacy and data flow

 - Audio is sent to OpenAI only when you transcribe.
 - Optional post-processing sends transcript text to OpenAI.
 - Stopped recordings are stored locally in IndexedDB for retry reliability.
 - Successful recordings are deleted from local audio storage after completion.
 - Raw transcripts and structured notes are saved in your vault.
 - No telemetry, tracking, ads, or background analytics.

 ### Recommended validation after updating

 On mobile, test:

 - A short normal recording.
 - A recording with network disabled, then retry with Retry pending voice transcriptions.
 - The iOS Shortcut URL if you use Action Button capture.
 - Microphone permission behavior after reinstall/update.

releases.author: DenizOkcu
releases.description:
1.3.0

  Added

  - Post-processing checkbox in the recording modal — toggle smart formatting per recording without diving into settings. The checkbox defaults to your global setting and persists changes back to it.

  Changed

  - All UI text now follows sentence case per Obsidian plugin guidelines.
  - Post-processing is now controlled by the modal checkbox instead of only the global setting, giving per-recording control.
  - Improved error handling: VoiceMDError is now a proper Error subclass with instanceof checks instead of duck-typing.
  - Replaced vault.createFolder (requires Obsidian v1.4.0) with vault.create for compatibility back to v0.15.0.
  - Proper promise handling with void instead of floating promises.

  Fixed

  - Build errors, lint issues, and dead code removed.

releases.author: DenizOkcu

Fixes speaker recognition in Meeting Mode

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1

releases.author: DenizOkcu
releases.description:
## 🎯 What's New

### Meeting Mode Now Per-Recording

Meeting Mode has been moved from global settings to the recording modal, giving you **per-recording control** over speaker identification!

## ✨ Key Improvements

### 🎤 Flexible Recording Control
- **Per-Recording Choice**: Enable Meeting Mode only when you need it
- **Cost Optimization**: Use speaker diarization only for meetings, not for solo recordings
- **Faster Workflow**: Modal checkbox is right where you need it

### 🚀 Modern Transcription Models
- **Standard Mode**: Now uses `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe` for fast, accurate transcription
- **Meeting Mode**: Uses `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` for advanced speaker identification
- **Better Quality**: Latest OpenAI transcription models with improved accuracy

## 📋 Usage

### Standard Recording (Solo Notes)
1. Click voice recording icon
2. Leave "Enable Meeting Mode" **unchecked**
3. Record your voice note
4. Get clean transcription without speaker labels

```markdown
Here's my task list for today: review the pull request,
update documentation, and test the new feature.
```

### Meeting Recording (Multiple Speakers)
1. Click voice recording icon
2. **Check** "Enable Meeting Mode (Speaker Identification)"
3. Record your meeting or interview
4. Get transcription with speaker labels

```markdown
**Speaker 1:** Let's discuss the quarterly results.

**Speaker 2:** Revenue is up 15% compared to last quarter.

**Speaker 1:** That's excellent news. What about expenses?
```

## 🔧 Technical Changes

### Breaking Changes
**None!** This update is fully backward compatible.

### Model Updates
- **Standard Mode**: `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe` (fast, affordable)
- **Meeting Mode**: `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` (speaker identification)
- **Response Format**: `json` for both models

### Settings Migration
- Old `enableMeetingMode` setting is no longer used
- Settings UI toggle removed
- No migration required - existing settings load normally

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fixed API compatibility issues with newer transcription models
- Removed speaker labels from standard transcriptions
- Improved error handling for transcription API calls

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/compare/1.1.3...1.2.0

releases.author: DenizOkcu
releases.description:
### Auto-Start Recording

New Setting: Auto-Start Recording

Skip the manual click—enable auto-start recording in settings to begin capturing audio immediately when opening the voice recording modal. Perfect for quick voice notes and faster workflows.

  Technical Details:
  - Added autoStartRecording boolean setting (default: false)
  - Modal triggers MediaRecorder.start() on open when enabled
  - Settings UI: toggle control under "Auto-Start Recording"
  - Maintains existing recording safeguards and max duration limits

  Configuration:
  Settings → Voice MD → Auto-Start Recording (toggle on/off)

releases.author: DenizOkcu
releases.description:
### What's New

#### GPT Post-Processing

Enable optional GPT-powered formatting to transform raw transcriptions into well-structured markdown with proper headings, lists, and paragraphs.

  How it works:
  1. Whisper transcribes your audio (unchanged)
  2. GPT structures the raw text into formatted markdown
  3. Two files created: transcription-{timestamp}-raw.md and transcription-{timestamp}.md
  4. Structured text inserted at cursor (backward compatible)

  New Settings

  - Enable Post-Processing - Toggle GPT structuring (opt-in, disabled by default)
  - Chat Model - Choose your model (default: gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency)
  - Custom Formatting Prompt - Override default instructions (optional)

  Robust Error Handling

  - Graceful fallback to raw transcription if post-processing fails
  - User-friendly error messages (rate limits, invalid models, network issues)
  - Progress notifications throughout the workflow

  ---
  Technical Details

  Architecture:
  - Clean separation: Whisper API → GPT API → File creation
  - Type-safe with proper error handling
  - Mobile compatible (iOS/Android)
  - Zero impact when disabled

  API Parameters:
  {
    model: "gpt-4o-mini",    // configurable
    temperature: 0.3,         // consistent formatting
    max_tokens: 4096         // handles long transcriptions
  }

  Performance: Adds ~2-5 seconds per transcription (GPT API latency)

  Cost: ~$0.01-0.05 per transcription (varies by model and length)

  ---
  Changelog

  Added

  - GPT post-processing with dual file creation
  - enablePostProcessing, chatModel, postProcessingPrompt settings
  - Voice Transcriptions/ folder with organized file naming
  - POST_PROCESSING_ERROR error type with fallback behavior

  Changed

  - Settings UI shows conditional fields based on post-processing toggle
  - Extended error handling for chat completion failures

  Breaking Changes: None. Fully backward compatible.

  ---
  Feedback: https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/issues | https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/discussions

releases.author: DenizOkcu
releases.description:
## 🎉 Now on Mobile!

  Voice MD now works seamlessly on **iOS and Android** devices. Same great features, now in your pocket.

  ## ✨ What's New

  📱 **Mobile Support**
  - iPhone & iPad (iOS 14.5+)
  - Android devices (modern Chrome/WebView)
  - Full feature parity with desktop

  🔧 **Enhanced Audio Format Detection**
  - iOS: MP4/AAC (native Safari format)
  - Android: WebM/Opus (native Chrome format)
  - Desktop: WebM/Opus (best quality)
  - Universal WAV fallback

  🎯 **Verified Compatibility**
  - Tested with Obsidian mobile's Capacitor WebView
  - No Node.js or Electron dependencies
  - Pure web APIs for maximum compatibility

  ## 📋 Changelog

  ### Added
  - Mobile platform support (iOS & Android)
  - Platform-specific audio format handling
  - Enhanced MIME type detection algorithm

  ### Changed
  - Updated `manifest.json`: `isDesktopOnly: false`
  - Improved audio format fallback chain

  ### Technical
  - Enhanced `getSupportedMimeType()` for mobile formats
  - Verified MediaRecorder API in Capacitor WebView

  ## 🚀 Installation

  ### BRAT (Recommended)
  Already have v1.0.0? BRAT will auto-update!

  New install:
  Settings → Community Plugins → BRAT
  Add Beta Plugin → DenizOkcu/voice-md

  ### Manual
  Download the three files below and place in `.obsidian/plugins/voice-md/`

  ## 💡 Perfect Mobile Use Cases

  - 🚶 On-the-go note capture during walks
  - 📱 Hands-free meeting notes
  - ⚡ Quick brain dumps without typing
  - 🌍 Multilingual input (90+ languages)

  ## ⚠️ Mobile Considerations

  **Works Great:**
  ✅ Microphone permissions (handled by OS)
  ✅ Recording quality (excellent)
  ✅ Network reliability (error handling included)
  ✅ UI responsiveness (adapts to screen)

  **Be Aware:**
  📶 Requires internet (API calls)
  🔋 Battery usage (recording + transcription)

  ## 🐛 Known Issues

  None! But this is the first mobile release—please report any issues you encounter.

  ## 🔗 Links

  - **Full Changelog**: [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md)
  - **Report Issues**: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/issues)
  - **Documentation**: [README.md](https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/blob/master/README.md)

  ---

  **Built with** TypeScript | **Powered by** OpenAI Whisper | **Made for** Obsidian Desktop & Mobile 📱

releases.author: DenizOkcu
releases.description:
Transform your voice into markdown text instantly with OpenAI Whisper. No context-switching, no copy-paste—just talk and watch your words appear.

  ## ✨ Features

  🎙️ **One-Click Recording** - Ribbon icon or command palette
  🤖 **Whisper-Powered** - State-of-the-art speech recognition
  ⚡ **Instant Insertion** - Text appears at cursor position
  🎨 **Real-Time Feedback** - Visual timer and status indicators
  🔒 **Privacy-First** - Ephemeral audio, no local storage
  🌍 **Multilingual** - Auto-detects 90+ languages

  ## 🚀 Quick Start

  1. Get an API key: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
  2. Install via BRAT: `DenizOkcu/voice-md`
  3. Paste key in settings → Start recording

  ## 💡 Perfect For

  - Brain dumps when typing feels slow
  - Meeting notes while your hands are busy
  - Multilingual note-taking
  - Accessibility-first workflows

  ## 📦 Installation

  ### BRAT (Recommended)
  Settings → Community Plugins → BRAT
  Add Beta Plugin → DenizOkcu/voice-md

  ### Manual
  Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` below
  Place in `.obsidian/plugins/voice-md/` → Restart Obsidian

  ## ⚙️ Requirements

  - Obsidian v0.15.0+
  - Desktop (Windows/Mac/Linux)
  - OpenAI API key (~$0.006/min)
  - Microphone access

  ## 🛣️ Roadmap

  - Mobile support
  - Local Whisper models (offline mode)
  - Custom prompt templates
  - Edit-before-insert option

  ## 🐛 Feedback

  First release! Your use cases will shape what's next.

  **Issues/Ideas:** https://github.com/DenizOkcu/voice-md/issues
  **Love it?** Star the repo ⭐

  ---

  Built with TypeScript | Powered by OpenAI Whisper | Made for Obsidian
